reasoning: A 1% increase in an Elo rating of 2453 is ~25 points. Leya has peaked above 2400 so her K-factor is strictly 10. Gaining 25 points requires a +2.5 outperformance of expected scoring, which is exceedingly rare in one month (empirical base rate of 0.007). FIDE ratings only update exactly once per month (on the 1st), meaning the November 2 and November 25 evaluations will almost certainly be exactly the same. The probability remains at the minimum allowed (0.02) for the first two dates. By January 24, there are 3 months of rating updates possible, making such a jump slightly more feasible, so the probability is increased marginally to 0.04 reflecting increased uncertainty over the horizon.
